title: To Surveil With Love

jokes.text: Duffman is here to refill your beers! Now, for the only thing better than Duff. Better than Duff? Free Duff stuff!
jokes.setup: Duffman claims something is better than Duff beer
jokes.punchline: It's just free Duff merchandise
jokes.explanation: Classic Duffman character beat - the corporate mascot's twisted logic that free branded merchandise is better than the actual product

jokes.text: And now, Duffman has a dinner date with his estranged daughter. Must not bring up why she dropped out of college. It's too sad!
jokes.punchline: Reveals tragic personal backstory while maintaining character voice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - takes the one-note corporate mascot and gives him genuine pathos while never breaking character. The specificity of 'why she dropped out' makes it funnier than generic sad backstory.

jokes.text: It's your pet canary. Now scram!
jokes.setup: Child asking if yellow gelatin is lemon or pineapple
jokes.punchline: Lunch lady reveals it's a dead pet canary
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and dark absurdism. The casual delivery of horrific information is classic Simpsons

jokes.text: First of all, Milhouse fell because you tripped him. Secondly, spaz is short for spastic diplegic, someone who suffers from a serious medical condition. Thirdly, your 'haw haw,' through overuse, has lost its power.
jokes.setup: Nelson bullying with his signature laugh
jokes.punchline: Lisa's overly academic deconstruction of his insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - her intellectual approach to playground bullying. The 'haw haw has lost its power' is particularly clever meta-commentary

jokes.text: Wow. I bow before your logic. (grunts)
jokes.punchline: Nelson's uncharacteristic respectful response followed by physical grunt
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Nelson's occasional moments of self-awareness, undercut by his return to physical comedy

jokes.text: Well, it's the one extracurricular activity that can't be cut, because it requires no equipment. We're low on funding, so Ralph Wiggum will be standing in as your lectern.
jokes.setup: Debate team explanation about no equipment needed
jokes.punchline: Ralph as human lectern due to budget cuts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - starts with reasonable premise (debate needs no equipment) then absurdly reveals Ralph as furniture substitute

jokes.text: I'm a furniture!
jokes.setup: Ralph being used as a lectern
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic acceptance of being objectified
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph moment - his innocent joy at being used as furniture captures his character perfectly. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: The sun and the breeze-- the two things you know a lot about. Right, Blondie?
jokes.punchline: Megan turns it into blonde stereotype attack
jokes.explanation: Decent setup for the episode's blonde prejudice theme, but relies on obvious stereotype humor

jokes.text: Why don't we get a bitchin' new perm with daddy's credit card?
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated valley girl impression
jokes.explanation: Generic valley girl stereotype humor - functional but unoriginal

jokes.text: First of all, my father no longer has any functioning credit cards.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact response about family financial troubles
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's practical nature and the Simpsons' financial reality cutting through the stereotype

jokes.text: Did they jump on the pile to smoosh it down?
jokes.punchline: Burns suggests childish solution to nuclear waste problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - treating catastrophic nuclear waste crisis with the logic of a garbage can

jokes.text: Eh, just find the biggest idiot on our payroll and slip it in his bag.
jokes.setup: Need to dispose of dangerous plutonium
jokes.punchline: Burns casually suggests framing an employee
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns evil casualness - treating employee safety as expendable convenience

jokes.text: That's a bingo.
jokes.setup: Burns seeing Homer as the perfect patsy
jokes.punchline: Burns' anachronistic exclamation
jokes.explanation: Decent Burns character beat with his outdated slang

jokes.text: The best mac and cheese I've ever had is at a bowling alley in Ogdenville. Breadcrumbs on the top. I'm on my way!
jokes.setup: Casual conversation about mac and cheese
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately abandons his current meal to drive to another town
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer impulsiveness - the specific detail about breadcrumbs triggering immediate travel plans

jokes.text: Well, now, why can't it be blown up by a woman of color?
jokes.setup: Bomb squad preparing to detonate suspicious bag
jokes.punchline: Someone prioritizing diversity representation over bomb disposal urgency
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of identity politics taken to absurd extremes during crisis situations

jokes.text: No time for a full countdown.
jokes.punchline: Casual abbreviation of dramatic countdown
jokes.explanation: Good economy - deflates action movie tension with bureaucratic efficiency

jokes.text: Enough radioactivity was released to create 17 Hulks and a Spider-Man.
jokes.punchline: Measuring radiation in superhero units
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist measurement system that makes radiation relatable through pop culture. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Everything I've said about terrorism till now was fear-mongering. But today, I monger the truth.
jokes.punchline: Admitting to previous fear-mongering while claiming current honesty
jokes.explanation: Good political satire about selective honesty and the phrase 'monger the truth'

jokes.text: Does he have a cute accent? Why don't you be the judge, young lady? Ooh, he sounds just like Paddington Bear!
jokes.punchline: Comparison to children's book character
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Springfield residents' naivety about British culture

jokes.text: Flowers, guv'nor? A pretty for your buttonhole? 'Ello, 'ello, 'ello.
jokes.punchline: Overly aggressive flower sellers as major crime
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of British stereotypes and the contrast between trivial old problems and serious new ones

jokes.text: Well, I'm Wally of Wally's Framers, and this is exactly what I want.
jokes.setup: Question about what framers of Constitution would want
jokes.punchline: Picture frame shop owner answers literally
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'framers' - picture framers vs. Constitution framers. Good economy and misdirection.

jokes.text: That blonde girl thinks she's smart, like us dark hairs. Again with the blonde! I'm smart, too. I got Joe to promise to leave his wife when I turn 50.
jokes.setup: Claiming intelligence based on hair color
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates poor judgment with affair details
jokes.explanation: Perfect character irony - claims intelligence while revealing spectacularly bad relationship decisions

jokes.text: All in favor say cheese. Those opposed say, 'I hate America.'
jokes.punchline: Rigged vote forcing patriotic compliance
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about false choices and manufactured consent in democracy

jokes.text: All in favor say, 'I have sex with animals.' I do have sex with animals, but I am not in favor of term limits.
jokes.punchline: Cletus admits to bestiality but opposes on principle
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and character consistency - Cletus will admit to anything but still has political principles

jokes.text: We're moving to Rectangu-Land! Round up the family and let's roll!
jokes.punchline: Shape-based wordplay with 'round up'
jokes.explanation: Decent pun work in the fake children's book, though fairly obvious

jokes.text: Did you hear about the blonde who couldn't make ice? She lost the recipe.
jokes.punchline: Recipe needed for frozen water
jokes.explanation: Generic blonde joke - exactly the kind of lazy humor the episode is critiquing

jokes.text: Blonde guys aren't dumb. They're evil. Like in The Karate Kid and World War II.
jokes.punchline: Escalating from movie villain to Hitler
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from pop culture to historical extremes, typical Bart logic

jokes.text: A woman sunbathing topless on R-15! Uh, Chief, I think we got a misuse of police equipment on ZZ-99. Killjoy.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum caught using cameras for voyeurism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Wiggum's unprofessional behavior and Lou/Eddie's competence

jokes.text: He was just jogging in a tan sweat suit, Chief. Are you sure? I thought I saw his... That was a fanny pack, worn on the front. But there was a... Water bottle... But it was... Leaking.
jokes.setup: Wiggum thinks he sees naked jogger
jokes.punchline: Lou patiently explaining away each misidentified body part
jokes.explanation: Great escalating sequence with Wiggum's perverted imagination vs Lou's professionalism

jokes.text: Peer at the monitor, Be judgmental, and Jot it down. One way to remember that is A.B.C.: Always Be Considering P.B. and J. But the single most important rule is the four A's: Always Act According to A.B.C.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly convoluted mnemonic system
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of bureaucratic over-complication - acronyms within acronyms within acronyms

jokes.text: I've got two teens in a public park goin' at it like a couple of gibbons in the back seat of Noah's Ark!
jokes.punchline: Mixing biblical reference with animal behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character moment - his religious worldview creating bizarre mixed metaphors

jokes.text: But I feel a little like I'm the Tom Voyeur to these Huckleberry Sinners.
jokes.setup: Ned feeling guilty about watching teens
jokes.punchline: Literary pun combining Tom Sawyer and voyeurism
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ned wordplay that perfectly captures his literary knowledge and moral discomfort

jokes.text: Or you could correct the behavior yourself with our Nag-a-sonic speakers.
jokes.punchline: Device name combining 'nagging' with 'ultrasonic'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay and product naming that captures the authoritarian nature of the surveillance

jokes.text: What the hell was that?! I ain't lettin' you touch me with somebody watchin'. Don't push it, Kearney. I'm Jimbo! Make me look easy in front of the voice.
jokes.punchline: Concerned about reputation with omniscient voice
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing teen concerns about image even with invisible authority

jokes.text: That's not Sesame Street, that's a gay bar.
jokes.punchline: Marge realizing inappropriate content
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag setup about surveillance exposing inappropriate content to children

jokes.text: Dye! Dye! Dye! (maniacally laughing)
jokes.punchline: Shouting 'dye' like a battle cry
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's determined response to prejudice

jokes.text: Golf ball-sized hail! Whoo-hoo!
jokes.punchline: Homer celebrating destructive weather because he won the bet
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment - celebrating disaster for personal gain

jokes.text: Well, sir, how many times have you been blown up since the cameras went in? Zero times.
jokes.punchline: Absurd metric for success (not being blown up)
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of post hoc reasoning in security theater arguments

jokes.text: Don't use that dog as a ramp. Less radical... Less radical! Stop gleaming that cube!
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and absurd skateboarding corrections
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character work - his square sensibility applied to skateboarding culture

jokes.text: Whoa, a blind spot!
jokes.punchline: Discovering area without surveillance
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's surveillance plot - Bart's rebellious nature finding freedom

jokes.text: Experimenting with my butt.
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact response to inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Good economy and character moment - Bart's casual approach to mischief

jokes.text: My little Einstein.
jokes.punchline: Homer's proud misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting - misunderstanding misbehavior as genius

jokes.text: Welcome to Chaos Corners, the Pleasure Patch, Satan's Triangle-- the one place in town no cameras can see you, and no laws can touch you.
jokes.punchline: Multiple dramatic names for small backyard area
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with Bart's dramatic naming and the contrast between small space and grand claims

jokes.text: Yep, Springfield is cleaner than the Lord's hand towels.
jokes.punchline: Ned's characteristic religious comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character voice - finding religious metaphor for mundane observation

jokes.text: Koo-koo-roo! Koo-koo-roo! A phony bird call?! That means Marge is coming! Everyone, act legal!
jokes.punchline: Absurd warning system for approaching authority
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with the warning system and the phrase 'act legal'

jokes.text: Remember how you wanted me to get that expensive operation? Well, now I can afford a motorcycle!
jokes.setup: Homer earning money to pay for Marge's medical needs
jokes.punchline: Instead buying motorcycle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer selfishness - misappropriating medical funds for personal desires

jokes.text: I don't want to get Homer in trouble, but it's just plain wrong to use that blind spot to turn our backyard into a Mecca of misdemeanors!
jokes.setup: Marge's moral dilemma about reporting Homer
jokes.punchline: Alliterative description of backyard crimes
jokes.explanation: Good Marge character moment with nice alliteration and moral conflict

jokes.text: Well guess what? I am not a brunette! I'm a blonde! Roots!
jokes.setup: Lisa succeeding in debate due to assumed brunette intelligence
jokes.punchline: Dramatic revelation of her true hair color
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the episode's hair prejudice theme with Lisa's principled stand

jokes.text: Not all fat people are jolly. Would you be jolly if you thought Comic-Con was moving to Anaheim?
jokes.punchline: Specific nerd culture reference as source of unjolliness
jokes.explanation: Good specific reference that makes the stereotype challenge concrete and relatable

jokes.text: This building cut me off!
jokes.setup: Claim that not all old people are bad drivers
jokes.punchline: Grampa immediately proving the stereotype by blaming building
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing - immediately undercutting Lisa's point with classic Grampa absurdist logic

jokes.text: Who wants feet steak? De-meat those feet!
jokes.punchline: Disturbing food preparation involving feet
jokes.explanation: Adequately absurd and disturbing for the lawless zone concept

jokes.text: Don't burn that pile of ventriloquist dummies!
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd item being regulated
jokes.explanation: Good specific absurd detail that makes the surveillance overreach concrete

jokes.text: You see, Ned, your citywide nagging compressed all of our town's misbehavior into one small place. Creating a glittering diamond of super evil.
jokes.punchline: Poetic description of concentrated evil
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor combining scientific observation with poetic language - 'glittering diamond of super evil' is highly quotable

jokes.text: Like God created the devil. God created the devil? Finally, He did something cool.
jokes.punchline: Bart's appreciation for divine rebellion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's rebellious nature finding appeal in divine mischief

jokes.text: That's the worst sin of all, for some reason.
jokes.punchline: Ned's uncertainty about his own religious knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of Ned's usual religious certainty with honest uncertainty

jokes.text: Reminds me of my boy. Oh, Mummy, my cat's breath smells like cat food.
jokes.punchline: His own child being equally nonsensical
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing that Ralph-like behavior isn't uniquely American

tmdb_overview: A bomb squad mistakenly blows up Homer's unattended gym bag, releasing radiation into the city and authorities react by suspending civil liberties. Wiggum and his men install surveillance cameras around Springfield and round up suspected terrorists, including groundskeeper Willie, but when monitoring the nonstop flow of video imagery proves to be too much, Wiggum enlists concerned citizens to help keep the city safe. Meanwhile, Lisa becomes fed up with being blonde, so she dyes her hair a dark color.
